articles.title: WHAT’S A TRUST CENTER, AND HOW DOES IT HELP SECURITY?
THREAT ACTORS EXPLOIT ADOBE COLDFUSION CVE-2023-26360 FOR INITIAL ACCESS TO GOVERNMENT SERVERS
articles.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a cybersecurity advisory in response to the exploitation of CVE-2023-26360 by unidentified threat actors at a Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agency. At least two public-facing servers were compromised between June and July 2023. The advisory provides network defenders with tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs), indicators of compromise (IOCs), and methods to detect and protect against similar exploitation.

articles.title: DECEMBER ANDROID UPDATES FIX CRITICAL ZERO-CLICK RCE FLAW
articles.summary: Google has released the December 2023 Android security updates, which address 85 vulnerabilities, including a critical zero-click remote code execution (RCE) bug. The RCE bug, tracked as CVE-2023-40088, was found in Android's System component and can be exploited without additional privileges.

articles.title: P2PINFECT - NEW VARIANT TARGETS MIPS DEVICES
articles.summary: A new variant of the P2Pinfect malware has been discovered specifically targeting embedded devices based on 32-bit MIPS processors. The malware attempts to brute force SSH access to these devices, making it highly likely that routers and IoT devices will be infected.

articles.title: MICROSOFT CYBERSECURITY REFERENCE ARCHITECTURES
articles.summary: The Microsoft Cybersecurity Reference Architectures (MCRA) are shared as part of the Security Adoption Framework. This post describes how Microsoft's cybersecurity capabilities integrate with their own and third party platforms. The resource includes information on antipatterns, best practices, and threat trends, among other topics. It also includes detailed technical diagrams for various aspects of cybersecurity.

articles.title: PREVENTING ACCIDENTAL EXPOSURE OF AWS RESOURCES
DETECTING AITM PHISHING SITES WITH FUZZY HASHING
HUMBLE
articles.summary: A humble and fast security-oriented HTTP headers analyzer.

articles.title: IMDSPOOF
articles.summary: IMDSpoof is a tool that spoofs the AWS IMDS service running on an EC2 instance. The tool spoofs the security-credentials IMDS endpoint and redirects it to a local webserver. This webserver can utilize Thinkst Canary HoneyTokens to alert on user access.

articles.title: COPACETIC
articles.summary: Copacetic can be used to quickly patch a container without going upstream for rebuild based on vulnerabilities. This can be used to shorten incident response times because the time from vulnerability announcement to active exploitation continues to narrow.

articles.title: MICROSOFT CONFIRMS WINDOWS BUG RENAMES PRINTERS TO HP LASERJET M101-M106
articles.summary: Microsoft has confirmed a bug that causes all printers to be renamed to HP LaserJet M101-M106, leading to automatic installation of the HP Smart app on Windows 10 and 11 devices. This occurs even on systems that are not HP computers or printers.

articles.title: 15,000 GO MODULE REPOSITORIES ON GITHUB VULNERABLE TO REPOJACKING ATTACK
articles.summary: Security research firm Vulncheck claims that 15,000 Go module repositories on GitHub are vulnerable to an attack called repojacking, where bad actors take advantage of account username changes and deletions to create repositories with the same name and stage open-source software supply chain attacks. These repositories account for at least 800,000 Go module-versions.

articles.title: IAM ACCESS ANALYZER ADDS UNUSED ACCESS AND CUSTOM POLICY CHECKS
articles.summary: AWS IAM Access Analyzer has added a new analyzer to continuously monitor roles and users for permissions that are granted and not used. The custom policy checks can be integrated with a CI/CD tool to allow for reporting on whether a change will add any additional IAM permissions.

articles.title: A DECADE OF HAVE I BEEN PWNED
articles.summary: A post from Troy Hunt reflecting on and detailing the history of Have I Been Pwned on its 10th birthday.

articles.title: SCAN FOR TAILSCALE SECRETS WITH TRUFFLEHOG
articles.summary: Tailscale announced a collaboration with TruffleHog to add their secrets’ patterns to TruffleHog. The tool will now be able to detect exposed Tailscale secrets in source code.

articles.title: THE TORTOISE AND THE MALWAHARE
articles.summary: PWC discusses how they're tracking a highly capable Türkiye-nexus threat actor known as Teal Kurma.
